sections.vendors: No new monthly supplier list surfaced this week; the most recent confirmed public version remains the spring 2026 spreadsheet on hmgroup.com, with the list cadence appearing to have shifted from monthly to quarterly. Published figures still show ~554–600 commercial product suppliers across roughly 970–1,100 tier-1 factories, with China and Bangladesh the largest apparel production markets.
sections.sustainability: H&M's multi-year deal with Recover to integrate mechanically recycled RCotton into product lines (building on the 2024 collaboration) remains the headline materials story. The group is tracking toward 100% recycled or sustainably sourced materials by 2030, having reported ~89% recycled/sustainably sourced and ~29.5% recycled content. No new OCS/GOTS/GRS/ECOVERO-specific announcements this week.
sections.sourcing: Bangladesh remains a key market (~300 factories, ~$5bn/year) but local officials report lower work orders year-on-year amid demand softness, US tariff uncertainty, and Bangladesh's approaching LDC graduation (November 2026). India (~92 factories, denim strength, government incentives) is gaining competitiveness; Turkey (~115 factories) remains the go-to for jersey, denim and knitwear speed sourcing.
sections.strategy: H&M is executing a net reduction of ~80 stores in 2026 (~90 openings vs ~170 closures), with the group store count down to ~4,036 as of 31 May 2026 — the biggest net losses in Asia, Oceania and Africa. The Monki brand has been shut down, with parts of the assortment folded into Weekday. Q2 net sales were ~SEK 54.8bn, roughly flat in local currencies despite ~3% fewer stores.
sections.pricing: H&M launched aggressive July 2026 summer sale discounts of up to 75% off in the UK. Sister brands are matching: COS up to 50% off (through 20 July), Arket up to 60% off tailoring, & Other Stories up to 50% off — heavy summer clearance across the whole group.
sections.launches: Wardrobe.NYC x H&M launches globally on 6 August: monochrome tailoring, wool coats, blazers, washed denim and padded bombers. Arket's SS26 debut ready-to-wear collaboration with artist Laila Gohar (27 pieces) is in market, and the Stella McCartney x H&M collection (certified/recycled materials, launched 7 May) continues its 20th-anniversary run.
soWhat: Bangladesh order volumes are softening while India gains ground — suppliers with Indian denim capacity or Turkish quick-turn knits are better positioned for H&M's near-term allocations.
The Recover RCotton multi-year deal signals recycled-cotton content is becoming a baseline expectation; mills should have GRS-certified recycled cotton blends ready to quote.
Heavy 50–75% clearance across all group brands plus a shrinking store estate points to tight inventory discipline — expect cautious AW26 order volumes and continued price pressure.
